title: Sarepta Wins Full Approval and Label Expansion for DMD Gene Therapy
text:
Sarepta Therapeutics received FDA approval for an expanded indication of ELEVIDYS, marking a significant regulatory victory just one week after Pfizer's gene therapy program failed in late-stage development for the same indication. ELEVIDYS received traditional full approval for ambulatory patients aged 4 years and older with confirmed DMD mutations.

Timing and Competitive Context: The approval came at a critical moment for the DMD gene therapy field. Pfizer's program had just failed, making Sarepta the clear leader in approved DMD gene therapy options. This timing strengthened Sarepta's market position considerably.

Label Expansion Impact: The expanded indication made ELEVIDYS available to over 80% of Duchenne patients in the United States, compared to the initial 4-5 year old ambulatory population under accelerated approval. This expansion dramatically increased the addressable market.

Market Expectations: Investors had high expectations for ELEVIDYS sales following the label expansion. Peak sales estimates ranged from $2.7 billion to over $5 billion annually. Q2 2024 results showed strong early commercial uptake with over 1,000 antibody screening kit requests within weeks.

Accelerated to Traditional Approval: The conversion from accelerated approval to traditional full approval for the ambulatory population provided additional confidence in the product's safety and efficacy profile. Ongoing approval for non-ambulatory patients would depend on confirmatory trial results.

Strategic Implications: The approval positioned Sarepta as the dominant player in DMD therapeutics heading into the critical 2024-2025 commercialization phase. The company was expected to generate substantial revenue growth from ELEVIDYS expansion.
